comment.body:
the market cannot be taken directly on the bear/bull axis. 
It has three phases: market can be sidewaying, it can be mean reverting and it can be trending (as you could read in my previous educational post. Very important one)

comment.body:
if it would have been a trending phase, i could have been bullish or bearish, bcs there is a bear or bull momentum...
but how about a mean reverting, oscillating, or sidewaying market?
am i bullish or bearish on which metrics? spot? vol? time decay?

(for now, you can see that only bcs vol goes up, it doesn't inherently mean that market goes down, spot/vol beta can be positive and negative too)

comment.body:
so, in reality, there are different market regimes and market characteristics.
sometimes the market is obvious, and can be predicted with high confidence.
sometimes it has more risk, and a different character, requiring different strategical approaches (this is where different lvls and their meanings play crucial part in order to understand what the market is doing, depending on how it reacts to certain dynamics/shifts that can are generated at certain lvls)
comment.raw_body:

comment.body:
this is a more complex approach, i know, you have to THINK, and TAKE YOUR TIME.
but isn't it what I promised you and told you in my welcome email?
market is a complex thing. there is no "layman terms", or "tl;dr"... at least, not always.

Don't try to oversimplify things, otherwise you will lose the meaning, and will learn nothing. Take the effort instead, or stay a "layman", your choice, but then you will leave without any new knowledge.

comment.body:
And you can't deny it, bcs if you saw how I owned the market last year, you know.
also since Feb, when those 2-dimensional guys were bearish, I bought the dips, then bulls also got bagged on their calls. I said, no longe-term trades.
We rode it up to ATH. Then, I udnerstood the story... I explained why the Feb OpEx won't be supportive (despite plp were firm about a supprotive opex), and I went for long dated puts, bcs I knew this gonna go down from there. 
But I also recognized that this is a slow, low-vol grind lower, a decay, a mean reverting downward trend and bcs of mean reversion, there is better edge on the call side. 
we played the game the way that catching intraday bottoms on 0DTE, i also showed you how to do it. Market was owned... 
my puts are printing, but not as much as you would expect. I was right about the calls-side edge. And predicted the squeeze, and  catched the bottom of the squeeze

Futures are 🩸🩸🩸 during asia
this is why scaling out and stop-loss is crucial in an environment like this... so is patient. 
as @gogumappa once said: "with Trump, it's like FOMC every day" 
this leg down will likely revert back up, as MMs will be filled with negative deltas, and they will need to rebalance.
I will come up with the lvls premarket, until watch how it goes.
As said yesterday: don't rush.
It isn't necessary a trap
